entries.position_summary: The country strongly supports the expansion of peaceful nuclear technologies, viewing them as essential for peace and human well-being. It highlights growing global adoption of nuclear energy and broader applications in industry, agriculture, and healthcare.
entries.quote: Mr. Chair, The development of peaceful nuclear technologies is essential for the maintenance of peace and human well-being. More and more States include nuclear energy in their energy mix, make increasing use of peaceful atom in their industry, agriculture, healthcare and other key economic sectors.

entries.position_summary: Russia supports broad access to peaceful nuclear energy for NPT States Parties and advocates expanding international cooperation in this area.
entries.quote: STATEMENT by the Delegation of the Russian Federation at the First Session of the Preparatory Committee for the 11th Review Conference of the Parties to the Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons (Cluster III: peaceful uses of nuclear energy) Mr. Chair, Russia has consistently stood for a broad access to the benefits of peaceful nuclear energy for the States parties to the NPT and the development of international cooperation in this area.

entries.position_summary: The country emphasizes that a trusted, impartial, depoliticized, and technically robust IAEA safeguards system is essential to the NPT and to enabling peaceful nuclear uses. It warns that politicization or technical shortcomings in safeguards would undermine the NPT verification regime and broader non-proliferation efforts.
entries.quote: The IAEA safeguards system, which is pivotal for strengthening the NPT and the peaceful uses of nuclear energy, is one of the key elements of this regime. It is essential that the safeguards system be trusted by all States parties to the Treaty. To secure confidence, it should be impartial, politically unbiased, technically reliable and sound. Failure to meet these criteria, as well as any attempts to politicize the IAEA safeguards can severely impair both the NPT verification regime and the nuclear non-proliferation in general.

entries.position_summary: Russia advocates broad access to peaceful nuclear technology and actively supports global nuclear energy development. It highlights exports of Russian-designed power units to multiple countries and the recent commercial operation of a plant in Belarus.
entries.quote: Russia actively promotes the development of nuclear energy across the world, and champions broad access of States to the benefits of the peaceful atom. Russian- designed power units are currently build in China, India, Egypt, Turkey, Bangladesh and Hungary. Late last year, a nuclear power plant in Belarus started commercial operation.

entries.position_summary: Russia supports broad access for NPT States Parties to the benefits of peaceful nuclear energy and advocates enhanced international cooperation in this domain.
entries.quote: Unofficial translation STATEMENT by the Deputy Head of the Delegation of the Russian Federation at the Third Session of the Preparatory Committee for the 11th Review Conference of the Parties to the Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons (Cluster III: Peaceful uses of nuclear energy) New York, 6 May 2025 Mr. Chair, Russia has consistently stood for the NPT States parties’ broad access to the benefits of peaceful uses of nuclear energy and the development of international cooperation in this area.

entries.position_summary: The country emphasizes its leadership in civil nuclear energy, citing 42 operational reactors (34.6 GW), including the world’s only floating power unit, providing 17.8% of national electricity and up to 40% in some regions. This presents its nuclear program as focused on peaceful energy production and market leadership.
entries.quote: Our country is among the leaders of the world nuclear energy market. A total of 42 nuclear power reactors with a total installed capacity of 34.6 GW are in operation at 12 Russian NPPs, including the world's only floating power unit of the Akademik Lomonosov nuclear thermal power plant; this accounts for 17.8 per cent of the total energy generated in the country (and up to 40 percent of energy generated in certain regions).

entries.position_summary: Russia prioritizes scientific research and development of radiation technologies and the peaceful uses of nuclear energy across medicine, agriculture, industry, and other economic sectors.
entries.quote: Mr. Chair, Russia's priorities include scientific research into and development of radiation technologies and peaceful uses of nuclear energy in medicine, agriculture, industry and other key economic sectors.

entries.position_summary: Russia highlights its leadership in peaceful nuclear applications by producing most of the world's medical isotopes, constructing Europe's largest radiopharmaceuticals plant in Obninsk, and promoting BRICS cooperation in nuclear medicine. This underscores a focus on expanding international collaboration and benefits of nuclear technology for health.
entries.quote: In the field of nuclear medicine, Russia produces over 70 per cent of the world's range of medical isotopes. The construction of Europe's largest radiopharmaceuticals plant is underway in Obninsk. We are working on a BRICS nuclear medicine cooperation platform.

entries.position_summary: The country promotes nuclear energy as a sustainable, low-carbon source that provides reliable clean power and significantly reduces greenhouse gas emissions. It views nuclear power as an essential tool for addressing climate change.
entries.quote: Nuclear energy is consistent with the principles of sustainable development and ensures steady supply of clean energy demonstrating the lowest life cycle СО 2 emissions. Its contribution to addressing climate change can hardly be overestimated. Nuclear power plants operated across the world save the world greenhouse gases emissions comparable to the quantity of carbon that can be absorbed by all the planet's forests.

entries.position_summary: Russia emphasizes the importance of nuclear energy for sustainable development and advocates broad access to peaceful nuclear technology. It highlights extensive global construction of Russian-designed power units and ongoing projects in several countries as evidence of its commitment.
entries.quote: Mr. Chairman, Russia considers nuclear energy as an important means of achieving the United Nations' sustainable development goals. Our country has always actively promoted the development of nuclear energy across the world, and championed broad access of States to the benefits of the peaceful atom. 110 power units based on USSR and Russian design have been constructed at nuclear power plants across the world. Our specialists are building 22 more high-power units in seven countries. In Turkey, four generation III+ 1200 MW power units are simultaneously constructed at Akkuyu nuclear power plant. Works to build nuclear power plants have been commenced in Hungary, Egypt and Bangladesh. New power units are under construction in China and India.

entries.position_summary: Russia highlights its support for peaceful nuclear technology by initiating and funding the IAEA’s INPRO, facilitating international collaboration and assessments of advanced nuclear systems, including Generation IV reactors. It emphasizes INPRO’s broad membership and role as a platform for information exchange and joint studies.
entries.quote: Russia has initiated and been a donor of the IAEA International Project on Innovative Nuclear Reactors and Fuel Cycles (INPRO), which has become an advanced centre for comprehensive assessment of nuclear power systems using an extensive range of criteria. At this platform, member States can exchange information and engage in joint studies in the field of advanced nuclear technology, including Generation IV nuclear energy systems. At the moment, INPRO membership consists of 43 countries.

entries.position_summary: Russia highlights its support for the IAEA’s peaceful applications of nuclear technology and contributes political, financial, and expert assistance, including donations to the IAEA Technical Cooperation Fund.
entries.quote: We provide continuous political, financial and expert support to the IAEA in its activities in the field of energy and non-energy applications of nuclear technology. Russia is a donor of the Technical Cooperation Fund.

entries.position_summary: The country advocates for nuclear power as a sustainable, clean, and reliable energy source and highlights concrete projects using nuclear technology to cut emissions. It claims Russian-designed nuclear power plants prevented 217 million tons of CO2 emissions in 2022, about half within Russia.
entries.quote: We have no doubt that modern nuclear power industry is consistent with the principles of sustainable development and provides the economy with stable and clean energy. We are implementing concrete projects to reduce emissions in various economic sectors using nuclear technology. Owing to all operating Russian-designed NPPs in the world, 217 mln tons of CO2 emissions were prevented in 2022, half of them in Russia.

entries.position_summary: Russia highlights its development and deployment of small- and medium-power civilian reactors, including an operating floating nuclear power plant in Pevek that supplies heat to most homes, and plans to deploy additional units. This underscores support for expanding peaceful nuclear energy applications.
entries.quote: Russia is developing small- and medium- power reactors. The world's only floating nuclear power station with two small-power reactors is successfully operating in Pevek, Chukotka, and provides heat to almost three quarters of the town's houses. There are future plans to deploy four more modernized power units.

entries.position_summary: The country emphasizes support for peaceful nuclear applications, assisting states in establishing national centers of nuclear science and technology. It cites opening a cyclotron facility in Bolivia to produce radiopharmaceuticals for medical diagnostics.
entries.quote: We assist States in applying nuclear technologies for non-energy purposes, in particular, in the construction of national centres of nuclear science and technology (CNST). Such cooperation is underway with a number of countries. In Bolivia, for example, a cyclotron complex for the production of radiopharmaceuticals has been opened this year. This industrial facility is capable of providing reliable diagnosis of a wide range of diseases for 5,000 patients a year.

entries.position_summary: Russia supports the IAEA’s efforts to expand the peaceful use of nuclear energy and collaborates with the Agency on developing nuclear energy infrastructure for newcomer countries. It highlights completion of a work cycle in 2022 and extensive training activities from 2017–2022.
entries.quote: Russia welcomes the IAEA policy to strengthen the development of nuclear energy. The implementation of the 2017 initiative to develop the nuclear energy infrastructure of newcomer countries is one of the major components of our interaction with the Agency aimed at achieving this goal. Another cycle of this work was completed in 2022. Around 60 training events with over 830 foreign experts participating were organized in 2017–2022.

entries.position_summary: Russia prioritizes research and development of radiation technologies and the peaceful use of nuclear energy across medicine, agriculture, industry, and other economic sectors, including cancer treatment.
entries.quote: Russia’s priorities include scientific research into and development of radiation technologies and peaceful uses of nuclear energy in medicine (including fight against cancer), agriculture, industry and other key economic sectors.

entries.position_summary: Russia supports the IAEA’s efforts to expand peaceful nuclear energy and actively contributes through the 2017 initiative to develop newcomer countries’ nuclear infrastructure. It highlights completing another work cycle in 2022 and organizing 60 training events for over 830 foreign experts.
entries.quote: Russia supports the IAEA policy to strengthen the development of nuclear energy. The implementation of the 2017 initiative to develop the nuclear energy infrastructure of newcomer countries is one of the largest components of our interaction with the Agency aimed at achieving this goal. Another cycle of this work was completed in 2022. In 2017–2022, 60 training events were organized with over 830 foreign experts participating.

entries.position_summary: Russia prioritizes peaceful nuclear applications in medicine, agriculture, and industry and supports IAEA nuclear applications. It provided voluntary contributions to the ReNuAL/ReNuAL+ project to modernize the IAEA’s Seibersdorf laboratories in 2015 (~€100,000), 2019 (€100,000), and 2021 (€100,000).
entries.quote: Russia’s priorities include scientific research into and development of radiation technologies and the peaceful uses of nuclear energy in medicine, agriculture, industry and other key economic sectors In particular, as part of its support for IAEA efforts in the area of nuclear applications, Russia made three voluntary contributions to the ReNuAL project to modernize the Agency's research laboratories in Seibersdorf (Austria): in 2015 - 5 mln roubles (about 100,000 Euros), in 2019 - 100,000 Euros for the implementation of ReNuAL and ReNuAL+, and 100,000 Euros in 2021.

entries.position_summary: Russia expresses support for IAEA initiatives related to cancer control, indicating backing for the peaceful applications of nuclear technology in health care. This suggests endorsement of IAEA programs like radiotherapy and diagnostics support.
entries.quote: Russia actively supports IAEA activities in the field of cancer control.

entries.position_summary: The country highlights participation in the IAEA’s Programme of Action for Cancer Therapy since 2016, signaling support for and utilization of peaceful nuclear applications in health care. This reflects engagement with IAEA technical cooperation for civilian benefits.
entries.quote: In 2016, we joined the Agency’s Programme of Action for Cancer Treatment.

entries.position_summary: The country strongly supports the peaceful use of nuclear technology as a tool to achieve the UN Sustainable Development Goals and calls for enhanced international cooperation to advance these applications.
entries.quote: Mr. Chairman, Nuclear technology is a reliable and proven tool for achieving the UN sustainable development goals. International cooperation is crucial to the success in achieving this goal and should be promoted and enhanced in every way possible.

entries.position_summary: Russia links the development of peaceful nuclear energy to robust nuclear safety and supports strengthening nuclear safety worldwide. It emphasizes that states bear sole responsibility for ensuring nuclear safety on their own territories and determining its parameters at their discretion.
entries.quote: The development of peaceful uses of nuclear energy is impossible without an adequate level of nuclear safety. The Russian Federation supports the strengthening of nuclear safety around the world, while respecting the fundamental principle, according to which states themselves should bear sole responsibility for ensuring nuclear safety on their territories and determine its optimal parameters at their discretion.

entries.position_summary: Russia advocates broad, non-discriminatory access to peaceful nuclear energy for all NPT-compliant states and promotes international cooperation in this area. It opposes unilateral restrictions and other barriers—framed as politicization—that it argues violate Article IV’s guarantee of equal access to nuclear materials, equipment, and technology for peaceful purposes.
entries.quote: Russia has consistently supported a broad access to the benefits of peaceful nuclear energy for the States parties to the NPT and the development of international cooperation in this area. All countries compliant with their obligations under the Treaty should have the right to freely develop peaceful nuclear technologies. Imposing unilateral restrictions, creating artificial barriers to bilateral and international cooperation, and undermining the principle of equal access to nuclear materials, equipment ant technical information to be used for peaceful purposes, violate Article IV of the NPT. Politicization of this area and creating artificial dividing lines are unacceptable.

entries.position_summary: Russia supports and promotes the development of nuclear energy, emphasizing the NPT’s peaceful uses pillar.
entries.quote: Mr. Chair, Russia continues to promote the development of nuclear energy.

entries.position_summary: The country highlights the widespread civilian applications of nuclear technology and its contribution to global energy production. This indicates support for promoting peaceful uses of nuclear science in sectors like medicine, agriculture, and industry.
entries.quote: Nowadays, nuclear energy accounts for about 10 percent of the world total energy production. Nuclear technologies have become an integral part of the life of people across the world. They are extensively used in medicine, agriculture, industry, that is, in almost every economic sector.

entries.position_summary: Russia supports the IAEA’s efforts to expand and improve the peaceful use of nuclear energy and contributes financially through the Agency’s Technical Cooperation Fund.
entries.quote: Mr. Chairman, We assist IAEA in its efforts to ensure more efficient and wider use of nuclear energy to maintain peace, health and well-being across the world. Russia is a donor of the Agency’s Technical Cooperation Fund.

entries.position_summary: The country highlights progress in constructing nuclear power plants in multiple states, indicating strong support for expanding peaceful nuclear energy and international cooperation in civilian nuclear projects. It portrays these developments as achievements in the peaceful use of nuclear technology.
entries.quote: The latest achievements include the following: in 2022, the first concrete was poured at the sites of unit 4 of Akkuyu NPP (Turkey), units 1 and 2 of El Dabaa NPP (Egypt), unit 8 of Tianwan NPP and unit 4 of Xudapu NPP (China). The Hungarian nuclear authority issued a permit for the construction of two power units at Paks II NPP. Construction of 4 units of Kudankulam NPP (India) and 2 units of Ruppur NPP (Bangladesh) is in full swing.

entries.position_summary: The country urges that the NPT review process devote greater attention to peaceful uses of nuclear energy. It highlights its own deployment of advanced nuclear power technologies as a driver of social and economic development.
entries.quote: It is our firm belief that the review process should pay more attention to the matters of the peaceful uses of nuclear energy. Our States are a vivid example of how the creation of nuclear power generating capacities based on advanced technologies contributes to social and economic progress.

entries.position_summary: The country emphasizes expanding peaceful nuclear energy by developing land-based small-scale projects, specifically a RITM reactor-based small nuclear power plant in Yakutia.
entries.quote: Alongside with that, we continue to develop land-based small-scale energy projects. The work on a RITM reactor-based small nuclear power plant is underway in Yakutia.

entries.position_summary: The country highlights extensive international cooperation in civilian nuclear energy, noting active construction of 22 power units across seven countries and a broader portfolio including both large and small-capacity NPPs using RITM reactors. This underscores support for expanding peaceful nuclear power projects abroad.
entries.quote: We are currently building 22 power units in seven countries (Bangladesh, Hungary, Egypt, India, Iran, China and Türkiye). The portfolio of our foreign projects totals 33 power units of high-capacity nuclear power plants in ten countries and six power units of small-capacity NPPs using RITM reactors in

entries.position_summary: Russia highlights ongoing civil nuclear cooperation with China, including delivering equipment for the CDFR fast reactor and collaborating on new Tianwan and Xudapu power units. This reflects support for expanding peaceful nuclear energy projects and exports.
entries.quote: Russia continues to implement the package of strategic cooperation with China. In late 2024, it delivered the final batch of equipment for the Demonstration Fast Reactor (CDFR). The countries are also working together on the construction of Tianwan NPP units 7 and 8 and Xudapu NPP units 3 and 4.

entries.position_summary: Russia highlights its leadership and financial support for the IAEA’s International Project on Innovative Reactors and Fuel Cycles (INPRO), underscoring its commitment to advancing peaceful nuclear technology. It emphasizes broad international participation, noting that 46 countries have joined the project.
entries.quote: Russia is the initiator and a donor of the IAEA International Project on Innovative Reactors and Fuel Cycles. Today, 46 countries have joined this project.

entries.position_summary: The country argues that all NPT-compliant states should have unrestricted access to peaceful nuclear technology and applications. This underscores support for equal access under the NPT’s peaceful uses pillar for compliant parties.
entries.quote: Mr. Chair, All NPT-compliant countries should have unrestricted access to peaceful atom.

entries.position_summary: Russia highlights its development and deployment of civilian small and floating nuclear power plants to provide energy in remote areas, noting operational experience with the first floating nuclear thermal plant and plans for small land-based reactors.
entries.quote: Russia has been working on small and medium power reactors, including floating nuclear power plants, which are especially needed and efficient in remote areas. Russia has been operating the world's first floating nuclear thermal power plant and implementing a project of a small nuclear power plant on land.

entries.position_summary: Russia emphasizes the benefits of nuclear energy for sustainable development and low-carbon power, highlighting its extensive domestic nuclear power program. It underscores ongoing investments in advanced civil nuclear technologies, including fast reactors, closed fuel cycle projects, floating plants, and small modular reactors.
entries.quote: Mr. Chair, This year marks the 70th anniversary of the industrial use of the energy released from the nucleus of an atom. In 1954, the world's first nuclear power plant was launched in the town of Obninsk in the USSR. Today, nuclear power accounts for 10 percent of the world total energy production, and nuclear technologies have become an integral part of the life of people across the world. Nuclear energy promotes the sustainable development goals and supplies economy with sustainable and clean energy with the lowest levels of СО emissions during the life cycle. 2 At the moment, Russia operates 42 power units at 12 nuclear power plants with a total installed capacity of 35 GW. Our country has engaged in extensive work on cutting-edge technologies. We run two fast-neutron nuclear reactors; the construction of a lead-cooled fast reactor is under way, which is part of the nuclear power facility intended to close nuclear fuel cycle within a single site; we operate the world's first floating nuclear thermal power plant with two small reactors; a land- based NPP with small reactor is currently under construction, and the programme of construction of small reactors is to be further expanded. Besides, Russia is the only

entries.position_summary: Russia highlights the significant and growing role of civilian nuclear power, noting it supplied about 20% of national energy in 2021 and achieved a record 223.4 billion kWh in 2022. This underscores support for expanding peaceful nuclear energy generation.
entries.quote: Back in 2021, the share of nuclear generation was about 20 percent of all energy produced in Russia. In 2022, an absolute record was set for electricity generation over the entire history of our nuclear industry – 223.4 bln kWh.

entries.position_summary: Belarus highlights the completion of its first nuclear power plant and the commissioning of a second unit connected to the national grid, emphasizing its development of peaceful nuclear energy. The statement underscores progress in civilian nuclear power generation.
entries.quote: In Belarus we have effectively completed the construction of the country's first nuclear power plant. This year, the second power unit was commissioned and connected to the unified energy system.

entries.position_summary: The country highlights ongoing construction of a BREST-300 fast neutron reactor with an integrated on-site closed fuel cycle, emphasizing advancement in civilian nuclear power infrastructure. It showcases commitment to developing innovative peaceful nuclear technologies.
entries.quote: The construction of a nuclear power unit with the BREST-300 fast neutron reactor in Seversk, Tomsk Region, is ongoing. First time in the world practice, a nuclear power plant with fast neutron reactors and on-site closed nuclear fuel cycle facilities will be built on the same site.

entries.position_summary: The country promotes peaceful nuclear energy, emphasizing small-scale nuclear power solutions. It highlights the successful operation of a floating nuclear power plant in Pevek that supplies heat to most homes and plans to deploy four additional modernized units.
entries.quote: We attach special importance to the development of small-scale power generation. In Chukotka, the world's only floating NPP with two low-power reactors is successfully operating in Pevek, which provides heat to almost three quarters of the town's houses. In the future, there are plans to deploy four more modernized power units.

entries.position_summary: Russia asserts leadership in constructing nuclear power plants abroad, emphasizing its active role in international cooperation on peaceful nuclear energy.
entries.quote: Russia remains the leader in the construction of nuclear power plants abroad.

entries.position_summary: The country emphasizes assisting States in applying nuclear technologies for non-energy purposes, notably by constructing Centers of Nuclear Science and Technology. It highlights cooperation with Bolivia, where a cyclotron-radiopharmaceutical complex and an irradiation center are operational and a research reactor is under construction.
entries.quote: We assist States in the application of nuclear technologies for non-energy purposes, in particular in the construction of national centers of nuclear science and technology (CNST). Bolivia has put into operation the pre-clinical cyclotron- radiopharmacological complex within such a Centre and a multipurpose irradiation centre; the research reactor is under construction. Cooperation in the construction 6

entries.position_summary: The country asserts that all NPT-compliant states have an unconditional right to access peaceful nuclear energy and expresses willingness to share its nuclear energy experience with partners.
entries.quote: Review Conference of the Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons in 2022: “All countries that comply with the provisions of NPT should have the right of access to peaceful nuclear energy without preconditions. We are ready to share our experience in nuclear energy with our partners.” 10

entries.position_summary: Russia highlights its civilian nuclear energy program, operating 12 nuclear power plants with 43 units, including a floating power unit and fast neutron reactors. It is expanding this capacity with a land-based low-capacity RITM-200 reactor plant and additional floating nuclear power plants under construction.
entries.quote: Russia currently operates 12 nuclear power plants with 43 power units, including the floating power unit of the Akademik Lomonosov nuclear thermal power plant and two advanced fast neutron nuclear reactors. A land-based low-capacity nuclear power plant with the RITM-200 reactor unit and new floating power plants are under construction.

entries.position_summary: Russia emphasizes expanding peaceful nuclear applications through the IAEA, supporting newcomer countries’ nuclear infrastructure, and leading the INPRO initiative on innovative reactors and fuel cycles. It promotes assured fuel supply and multilateral fuel-cycle approaches, and contributes to IAEA health initiatives such as PACT and Rays of Hope.
entries.quote: Russia considers the efforts to promote the nuclear energy development central to the IAEA work. We cooperate with the Agency in implementing the initiative launched in 2017 to develop the nuclear energy infrastructure of newcomer countries. Russia is the initiator and leading donor of the IAEA International Project on Innovative Reactors and Fuel Cycles, in which 43 countries and the European Commission participate. Together with the Agency, we are working on the development of the concept of assured supplies and multilateral approaches to nuclear fuel cycle. In 2016, Russia joined the IAEA's Programme of Action for Cancer Treatment and subsequently the Rays of Hope initiative.

entries.position_summary: The country asserts its Article IV right to develop nuclear energy and engage in nondiscriminatory international cooperation for peaceful purposes. It opposes unilateral restrictions, export barriers, and politicization that undermine equal access to nuclear materials, equipment, and technology.
entries.quote: 4 national nuclear energy policy and to engage in cooperation in this area without any discrimination. Imposing unilateral restrictions, inventing barriers for bilateral and multilateral cooperation, as well as undermining the principle of equal access to nuclear materials, equipment and technical information to be used for peaceful purposes violate Article IV of the NPT. Politicization and artificial dividing lines in this sphere are unacceptable.

entries.position_summary: Russia underscores that it uniquely operates a civilian nuclear-propulsion fleet, with seven nuclear-powered icebreakers active on the Northern Sea Route. This highlights the peaceful, civilian application of naval nuclear technology.
entries.quote: Besides, Russia is the only country running a fleet of civilian vessels with nuclear propulsion. Seven nuclear-powered icebreakers are currently operating on the Northern Sea Route, a major traffic artery connecting Europe, Russia and the Asia-Pacific.

entries.position_summary: Russia argues that compliant States should not face restrictions on access to peaceful nuclear technology, affirming Article IV rights and opposing discriminatory export limitations.
entries.quote: Russia finds unacceptable any attempts to limit the access to the benefits of peaceful atom for any State compliant with its non-proliferation obligations.

entries.position_summary: Supports non-discriminatory access to peaceful nuclear energy for all NPT parties and opposes unilateral restrictions on nuclear technology trade affecting both supplier and recipient states.
entries.quote: We stand for non-discriminatory access for all NPT States parties to the benefits of peaceful nuclear energy and the inadmissibility of any unilateral restrictions against both supplier and recipient countries of nuclear technology.

entries.position_summary: Russia emphasizes strengthening national capacity for the safe use of nuclear energy in cooperation with the IAEA. It is party to key international nuclear safety and emergency conventions and acceded to the Vienna Convention on Civil Liability for Nuclear Damage in 2005.
entries.quote: We have been consistently strengthening our national capacity to ensure the safe use of nuclear energy in extensive collaboration with the IAEA. Russia is party to the Convention on Nuclear Safety, the Convention on Early Notification of a Nuclear Accident and the Joint Convention on the Safety of Spent Fuel Management and on the Safety of Radioactive Waste Management, as well as to the Convention on Assistance in the Case of a Nuclear Accident or Radiological Emergency. In 2005 Russia acceded to the 1963 Vienna Convention on Civil Liability for Nuclear Damage.

entries.position_summary: The country argues that IAEA safeguards must be applied in a non-discriminatory manner and should not be used to restrict states’ legitimate right to peaceful nuclear technology. It emphasizes protecting access to peaceful uses while implementing safeguards.
entries.quote: We underline that the safeguards should not be a pretext for discrimination against certain countries with regard to the exercise of the right to use nuclear technology for peaceful purposes.

entries.position_summary: The country underscores expansion of its civilian nuclear energy program, commissioning Kursk-2 Unit 1 at minimum power and advancing new units at Smolensk-2 and Leningrad-2. This signals support for peaceful applications of nuclear technology.
entries.quote: Yet we intend to do more. This year the reactor of the Kursk-2 nuclear power plant's unit 1 is to be started in the minimum-power mode. We are also about to commence work on Smolensk-2 NPP support infrastructure. At Leningrad-2 NPP, the foundation of unit 3 has been laid.

entries.position_summary: The country highlights support for peaceful nuclear cooperation by establishing a BRICS-linked business platform to coordinate leading nuclear companies in developing and deploying nuclear energy and related technologies. This underscores a focus on collaborative advancement of civilian nuclear applications.
entries.quote: Last year, a nuclear energy business platform was established on the margins of BRICS in order to join efforts of the leading nuclear companies of the BRICS countries to develop and deploy nuclear energy and non-energy technologies.

entries.position_summary: The country promotes a closed nuclear fuel cycle using fast reactor technologies to enhance resource utilization and reduce radioactive waste toxicity and volume. It is constructing a lead-cooled fast reactor with an on-site closed fuel cycle as a demonstration of this approach.
entries.quote: We believe that the future of nuclear energy is inextricably linked to a closed nuclear fuel cycle with "fast" reactor technologies being its integral part. This would allow us to expand the resource base manifold and significantly decrease the volume and radio-toxicity of radioactive waste sent for final disposal. We continue the construction of the world's first power facility with a lead-cooled fast reactor and with an on-site closed nuclear fuel cycle. 1

entries.position_summary: Russia underscores its leadership and funding role in the IAEA’s INPRO initiative, signaling strong support for advancing peaceful nuclear power systems and fuel cycles under IAEA auspices.
entries.quote: Russia is not only an initiator but also a leading donor of the IAEA International Project on Innovative Nuclear Reactors and Fuel Cycles (INPRO), which has become a fully operational mechanism and an advanced centre for comprehensive analysis of nuclear power systems.

entries.position_summary: Russia emphasizes its leadership and financial support for the IAEA’s INPRO initiative, portraying it as a fully operational center of excellence that advances comprehensive analysis and understanding of nuclear power systems. This underscores Russia’s support for international cooperation on peaceful nuclear technology development.
entries.quote: Russia is not only the initiator but also the leading sponsor/donor of the IAEA International Project on Innovative Nuclear Reactors and Fuel Cycles (INPRO), which has become a fully operational mechanism and a center of excellence in the comprehensive analysis of nuclear power systems. It takes into account a number of factors. This intellectual platform enhances understanding in 7

entries.position_summary: The country highlights extensive use of nuclear propulsion for civilian maritime purposes, operating a fleet of nuclear-powered icebreakers and a nuclear-powered cargo vessel. It emphasizes ongoing construction and operation of new-generation nuclear-powered icebreakers, underscoring commitment to civilian naval nuclear propulsion.
entries.quote: Over these years, we have put in operation eleven more nuclear-powered icebreakers and a nuclear-powered LASH Carrier “Sevmorput”. We continue building and operating new-generation nuclear-powered icebreakers and remain the only country in the world possessing a fleet of nuclear-powered icebreakers.

entries.position_summary: Russia highlights its major role as a global supplier of enriched uranium and nuclear fuel and emphasizes prioritizing research and development of civilian nuclear applications in medicine, agriculture, and industry. It also stresses capacity-building by training nuclear professionals, hosting thousands of foreign students in related fields.
entries.quote: Our country provides over a third of the world's supply of enriched uranium and is one of the leading nuclear fuel manufacturers. Russia's priorities include scientific research into and development of radiation technologies and peaceful uses of nuclear energy in medicine, agriculture, industry and other key economic sectors. We attach special importance to training professionals in nuclear and related spheres. Over two thousand students from 65 foreign countries are currently studying in Russian universities.

entries.position_summary: Russia argues that states complying with their non-proliferation obligations should not face restrictions on access to peaceful nuclear technology and benefits, implicitly criticizing restrictive export control practices that go beyond NPT obligations. It emphasizes the Article IV right to peaceful uses for compliant states.
entries.quote: Russia finds inadmissible any attempts to limit the access to the benefits of peaceful atom for any State compliant with its non-proliferation obligations.

entries.position_summary: They argue that non-proliferation measures, including export controls, should not unduly restrict legitimate trade in dual-use items or civil science and technology cooperation, emphasizing the protection of peaceful uses. They advocate a balanced approach that preserves legitimate commerce while upholding non-proliferation goals.
entries.quote: We believe that international non-proliferation efforts should not lead to unreasonable restrictions on legitimate trade in dual-use goods and technologies and on civil cooperation in the areas of science and technology.

entries.position_summary: The country condemns using the NPT as a pretext to restrict access to nuclear technology, emphasizing Article IV’s guarantee of the right to peaceful nuclear energy. It argues such restrictions are politically motivated abuses by some parties that create a false impression of the Treaty’s unfairness.
entries.quote: Another destructive trend is the use of the NPT as a pretext for restricting States' access to nuclear technology. Those who do so forget about Article IV of the NPT, which guarantees the right to peaceful uses of nuclear energy. This approach is also dangerous because it creates the false impression that the Treaty is unfair, when in fact it only concerns the abuse of the NPT provisions by some NPT parties who thus try to deal with their short-term political challenges.

entries.position_summary: Russia emphasizes advancements in small modular reactor development, signaling progress and leadership in peaceful nuclear energy technologies.
entries.quote: Russia has made considerable progress in the development of small modular reactors.

entries.position_summary: Russia argues that all NPT-compliant states should have unimpeded access to peaceful nuclear energy without additional conditions and expresses readiness to expand cooperation and share its expertise. This implies opposition to extra restrictions on nuclear trade beyond NPT obligations.
entries.quote: We note that all NPT-compliant countries should have access to peaceful nuclear energy without any additional conditions. We know that the overwhelming majority of States parties share this approach. Russia is ready to further impart its experience in nuclear energy with its partners and to enhance mutually beneficial cooperation.

entries.position_summary: Russia highlights its substantial civilian nuclear energy capacity, operating 42 reactor units at 12 nuclear power plants totaling 35 GW. This underscores its engagement in peaceful nuclear applications.
entries.quote: Mr. Chairman, At the moment, Russia operates 42 power units at 12 nuclear power plants with a total installed capacity of 35 GW.

entries.position_summary: The country intends to expand its civilian nuclear energy program by using water-cooled thermal reactors and breeder reactors to close the fuel cycle. This signals support for advanced peaceful nuclear technologies and fuel cycle closure.
entries.quote: Our country pursues a strategic goal of building a two-component nuclear- energy system consisting of water-cooled, water-moderated thermal-neutron power reactors and breeder reactors closing the nuclear fuel cycle.

entries.position_summary: The country underscores widespread deployment and growing foreign orders for its Russian-designed nuclear power plants, framing this expansion as aligned with UN long-term sustainable development goals and supportive of peaceful nuclear energy.
entries.quote: To date, 106 Russian-designed NPP units have been built worldwide. The portfolio of foreign orders now includes 34 power units. In these activities, we are oriented towards the objectives of long-term and sustainable development of the United Nations.

entries.position_summary: The country insists that compliant states must have unhindered access to peaceful nuclear technology and opposes any restrictions on this access. It condemns the political or economic instrumentalization of the NPT and pledges to counter such practices.
entries.quote: We reject any attempts to limit the access to the benefits of peaceful atom for any State compliant with its non-proliferation obligations. Consequently, we will do everything we can to counteract the abuse of the NPT to pursue political and economic tasks that are not directly related to the non-proliferation issues.

entries.position_summary: It criticizes certain countries for violating UNSCR 2231 and obstructing the JCPOA’s revival, aggravating tensions around Iran. It affirms Iran’s NPT-enshrined right to peaceful nuclear activities and claims Iran is complying with its obligations.
entries.quote: 3 violated UN Security Council Resolution 2231 and then disrupted the process of the revival of the 'nuclear deal'. The moves of these countries lead to further aggravation around Iran and its nuclear activities, fuelling instability in the Middle East. Iran's right to develop peaceful nuclear activities is enshrined in the NPT as the country is a party to the Treaty and strictly complies with its obligations.

entries.position_summary: Russia expresses strong support for IAEA technical cooperation, noting its financial contributions to the Technical Cooperation Fund and collaboration with the IAEA. It highlights joint work to remediate uranium legacy sites, underscoring commitment to addressing historical nuclear-related environmental impacts.
entries.quote: We highly appreciate the work of the IAEA Secretariat to encourage cooperation between States via the Department of Technical Cooperation and the Department of Nuclear Sciences and Applications. Russia is a donor of the Technical Cooperation Fund. Together with the IAEA, we are implementing a project to remediate uranium legacy sites.

entries.position_summary: The country highlights expansion of civilian nuclear energy by developing four upgraded 106 MW floating power units using RITM-200S reactors, building on the Akademik Lomonosov floating nuclear plant. This underscores support for deploying nuclear technology for peaceful power generation.
entries.quote: In addition to the Akademik Lomonosov floating nuclear thermal power plant, we are building a fleet of four upgraded 106 MW floating power units each having a RITM-200S reactor.

entries.position_summary: The delegation criticizes Western countries for using political measures to regain dominance in nuclear technology, arguing these actions undermine free trade and violate the NPT’s Article IV on non-discriminatory access to peaceful nuclear energy. They call for unhindered, non-politicized cooperation in the peaceful development of nuclear energy.
entries.quote: Mr. Chairman, We would like to note the reinforcement of negative tendencies in the development of nuclear energy. They are brought about by Western countries' attempts to regain the lost leading positions and competencies in nuclear technologies using political methods against their competitors. Such steps run counter to the principles of free trade, Article IV of the NPT and the purposes and

entries.position_summary: Russia highlights its leadership in uranium enrichment and nuclear fuel production and prioritizes expanding peaceful nuclear applications in medicine, agriculture, industry, and other sectors. It also emphasizes training and capacity-building, hosting thousands of international students in nuclear-related fields.
entries.quote: Russia is a leader in uranium enrichment and nuclear fuel production. Its priorities include scientific research into and development of radiation technologies and peaceful uses of nuclear energy in medicine, agriculture, industry and other key economic sectors. We attach special importance to training professionals in nuclear sphere: over two thousand students from 65 foreign countries are currently studying in Russian universities.

entries.position_summary: The state criticizes U.S.-led Western countries for using political and trade-restrictive measures against competitors in the nuclear sector, arguing these actions violate Article IV’s inalienable right to peaceful nuclear energy and contradict IAEA objectives and free trade principles. It calls for non-discriminatory access to peaceful nuclear technologies.
entries.quote: We would like to note the reinforcement of negative tendencies in the development of nuclear energy. Those are related to the fact that a group of States, first and foremost, the U.S.-led Western countries, use political methods against their competitors with considerably more advanced nuclear technologies. Such steps run counter to the principles of free trade, Article IV of the NPT and the purposes and objectives of the IAEA.

entries.position_summary: Russia highlights its unique civil nuclear marine propulsion program, noting seven nuclear icebreakers in service and additional vessels under construction, indicating ongoing expansion of peaceful nuclear-powered shipping capabilities.
entries.quote: Russia is the only country using civil nuclear-powered vessels. Currently, there are seven nuclear icebreakers in operation, and one nuclear transport vessel and three more icebreakers are under construction.

entries.position_summary: The country highlights the use and expansion of civilian naval nuclear propulsion, operating seven nuclear-powered icebreakers and constructing five more. This reflects support for peaceful applications of nuclear technology in maritime transport.
entries.quote: 6 country using civilian nuclear-powered vessels, with seven nuclear icebreakers in operation, and five more being constructed.

entries.position_summary: Russia advocates for adopting a closed nuclear fuel cycle in civilian nuclear energy to expand fuel resources and significantly reduce nuclear waste. It indicates Russia is actively developing and implementing this approach.
entries.quote: We note that the future lies in closing nuclear fuel cycle (NFC). It is the closed NFC that will enable us to expand the fuel reserve of the nuclear energy by tens of times and reduce the quantity of nuclear waste by many times. Russia has been working on this.

entries.position_summary: Belarus reports that its first nuclear power plant began commercial operation in November 2023, signaling its development of civilian nuclear energy. This underscores an emphasis on the peaceful uses pillar of the NPT.
entries.quote: 3 In November 2023, the first Belarusian nuclear power plant started commercial operation.

entries.position_summary: Russia highlights prioritizing the production and delivery of medical isotopes, emphasizing peaceful nuclear applications and supporting healthcare-related nuclear technology. This suggests a commitment to maintaining or expanding isotope supply capabilities.
entries.quote: Russia places special emphasis on the production and delivery of medical isotopes.

entries.position_summary: The country emphasizes strengthening national capacity for the safe use of nuclear energy and highlights active cooperation with the IAEA. This indicates support for peaceful nuclear applications coupled with robust safety measures.
entries.quote: We have been consistently strengthening our national capacity to ensure the safe use of nuclear energy in active collaboration with the IAEA.

entries.position_summary: Russia highlights support for peaceful nuclear development by establishing nuclear science and technology centers and providing training for the nuclear power workforce. It also emphasizes education, noting that over 2,000 students from 65 countries study nuclear-related disciplines at Russian universities.
entries.quote: 8 assistance, nuclear science and technology centers are being established. We actively participate in the training and retraining of personnel for the nuclear power industry. Over 2,000 students from 65 countries study at Russian universities specializing in nuclear and related disciplines.

entries.position_summary: The country argues that non-proliferation measures, including export controls, should not impose unjustified restrictions on legitimate dual-use trade and civilian scientific and technological cooperation. It supports a balanced approach that preserves access to peaceful nuclear-related technologies.
entries.quote: Mr. Chair, We believe that international non-proliferation efforts should not lead to unjustified restrictions on legitimate trade in dual-use goods and technologies and civilian scientific and technical cooperation.

entries.position_summary: Russia highlights its contribution to peaceful nuclear development by offering extensive international training and capacity-building through the Obninsk centre and Rosatom’s Technical Academy, including preparing operational staff for nuclear power plants it constructs abroad.
entries.quote: Russia has established Obninsk global centre for international nuclear training, where up to 10,000 people can gather for scientific, educational and youth events. Rosatom's Technical Academy has trained over 2,500 professionals from 95 countries. In addition, we have trained over 5,000 members of operational staff for the nuclear power plants that our country is building abroad.

entries.position_summary: The Kyrgyz Republic prioritizes developing its capacity to fight cancer, signaling support for peaceful nuclear applications in medicine (e.g., radiotherapy and nuclear medicine), likely in cooperation with the IAEA. This reflects an emphasis on leveraging nuclear technology for health benefits.
entries.quote: development of the Kyrgyz Republic's capacity to fight cancer.

entries.position_summary: Russia showcases advances in civilian nuclear power, noting it operates two fast reactors and that the BN-800 has moved to full MOX fuel, claimed as a world first. This emphasizes Russia's leadership and commitment to developing peaceful nuclear energy technologies.
entries.quote: 2 At the moment, Russia is operating two fast reactors. In 2022, the BN-800 reactor of the Beloyarsk NPP completely switched to uranium-plutonium fuel, that is, for the first time in the world a fast reactor started commercial operation using full load of MOX-fuel.

entries.position_summary: Russia states it is uniquely operating civil nuclear-powered vessels, specifically seven nuclear icebreakers and one nuclear transport ship currently in service.
entries.quote: Russia is the only country using civil nuclear-powered vessels. Currently, there are seven nuclear icebreakers and one nuclear transport vessel in operation.

entries.position_summary: Russia highlights progress on a Generation IV fast-neutron power plant with on-site fuel cycle, emphasizing reuse of spent fuel. It frames this as expanding nuclear energy’s resource base, improving environmental performance, and addressing radioactive waste management.
entries.quote: Russia has been successfully developing the project of construction of a Generation IV energy complex with a 300 MW BREST fast-neutron reactor and on-site nuclear fuel cycle facilities. In December 2024, a facility for fabrication and refabrication of unique mixed fuel for it went into pilot commercial operation. This has become a truly landmark event in the implementation of the project of the world's first Generation IV power plant allowing re-use of spent nuclear fuel. This approach will considerably expand the resource base of the nuclear energy, make it more environmentally friendly and offer solution for the issue of radioactive waste management.

entries.position_summary: The country emphasizes peaceful nuclear energy by developing transportable micro-reactors and plans to deploy a 10 MW Shelf-M unit by 2030 for grid-connected electricity generation.
entries.quote: Besides, we are developing a line of nuclear micro-reactors. By 2030, a 10 MW Shelf-M reactor is to be put in operation. It has capsule design and can be shipped anywhere to be subsequently connected to the local power grid.

entries.position_summary: Russia emphasizes its leadership in civilian nuclear fuel-cycle services and the export of nuclear power plants, presenting these activities as contributions to sustainable development and peaceful nuclear cooperation. It highlights a large global portfolio of Russian-designed reactors built and on order.
entries.quote: Russia holds a top position in uranium conversion and enrichment and remains the leader in the construction of nuclear power plants abroad. To date, 106 Russian-designed NPP units have been built worldwide. The portfolio of foreign orders now includes 34 power units. In these activities, we focus on United Nations long-term and sustainable development objectives.

entries.position_summary: The country advocates for a closed nuclear fuel cycle incorporating fast reactor technologies as essential to the future of nuclear energy. It argues this approach expands fuel resources and significantly reduces spent fuel waste, achieving radiological equivalence to the natural uranium consumed.
entries.quote: We believe that the future of nuclear energy is inextricably linked to a closed nuclear fuel cycle with "fast" reactor technologies being its integral part. This approach would expand the resource base and significantly reduce the accumulation of spent nuclear fuel waste, thereby achieving radiological equivalence to the natural uranium consumed by nuclear power.

entries.position_summary: The country is expanding peaceful nuclear energy by deploying an onshore advanced small modular reactor (RITM-200H) with a 40-year electricity supply plan from 2028 and constructing four floating power units (~110 MW each) to support industrial power needs. This underscores support for SMR and floating nuclear power applications for civilian electricity generation.
entries.quote: The agreement defines a 40-year period of electricity supply from the world's first onshore ASMM with a RITM-200H reactor unit from 2028. In 2022, the hulls of the first two floating power units (FPU) for the Baimskaya mining company were laid down. A total of four FPSUs with installed capacity of up to 110 MW each will be manufactured.

entries.position_summary: The country insists that only consensus-approved recommendations should guide decisions in the current NPT review cycle. It argues that outcomes on a Cluster 3 (peaceful uses) issue should be considered alongside other proposals at the 2026 Review Conference and assessed for their impact on the NPT review process and the Treaty overall.
entries.quote: We believe that recommendations that have not been approved by consensus cannot be viewed as a workable basis for decisions to be adopted within the current NPT review cycle. The outcome of discussions under cluster 3-specific issue should be considered together with the rest of the proposals that will be tabled at the 2026 Review Conference and assessed, inter alia, in terms of their influence on the Treaty review process and the NPT in general.

entries.position_summary: The country emphasizes cooperation with the IAEA to support newcomer states in developing nuclear infrastructure through capacity-building. It reports organizing 74 training events from 2017–2023 for over 1,200 foreign experts under this initiative.
entries.quote: The implementation of the 2017 initiative to develop nuclear infrastructure of the newcomer countries is one of the major components of our interaction with the Agency. 74 training events for over 1200 foreign experts were organized in 2017-2023 within the framework of this initiative.

entries.position_summary: Russia highlights its support for peaceful nuclear applications by constructing nuclear-powered icebreakers and assisting countries like China, Turkey, Egypt, India, and Bangladesh in building nuclear power plants and in non-energy nuclear technologies, emphasizing international cooperation in civilian nuclear development.
entries.quote: Three nuclear icebreakers are under construction. The Russian Federation assists other countries in building nuclear power plants. They include China, Turkey, Egypt, India and Bangladesh. We help in the application of peaceful nuclear technologies for non-energy purposes. In a number of States, with Russia's

entries.position_summary: Russia highlights progress in civilian nuclear energy using fast-neutron reactors: BN-800 now operates fully on MOX fuel, a BN-1200M unit is planned for completion by 2035, and MBIR has physically launched a BN-1200M core model. This underscores an emphasis on advanced nuclear power and fuel-cycle capabilities for peaceful purposes.
entries.quote: At the moment, Russia is operating two fast-neutron reactors. The BN-800 reactor of the Beloyarsk NPP has completely switched to MoX fuel. By 2035, the construction of a BN-1200M power unit is to be concluded at this power plant. At the MBIR site in Dimitrovgrad, a model of the BN-1200M reactor core has been physically launched.

entries.position_summary: The country highlights the launch of an export project to build a small nuclear power plant in Uzbekistan, signaling support for international cooperation and the expansion of civilian nuclear energy. This underscores a focus on facilitating peaceful nuclear technology deployment abroad.
entries.quote: May 2024 saw the launch of the world's first export project involving the construction of a small nuclear power plant of 6 power units in Uzbekistan.

entries.position_summary: Russia underscores its substantial civilian nuclear energy infrastructure, operating 43 power units at 12 nuclear power plants, including the floating Akademik Lomonosov. This highlights an emphasis on peaceful nuclear power generation.
entries.quote: A total of 43 power units are in operation at 12 Russian NPPs, including the floating power unit of the Akademik Lomonosov nuclear power station.

entries.position_summary: The statement highlights active construction of nuclear power plants in Turkey, Egypt, China, India, and Bangladesh, reflecting support for expanding civilian nuclear energy. It underscores a positive stance toward the NPT’s peaceful uses pillar.
entries.quote: In 2022, the first concrete was poured at the sites of unit 4 of the Akkuyu NPP (Turkey), units 1 and 2 of El Dabaa NPP (Egypt), unit 8 of the Tianwan NPP and unit 4 of the Xudapu NPP (China). The construction of 4 units of the Kudankulam NPP (India) and 2 units of the Ruppur NPP (Bangladesh) is in full swing.

entries.position_summary: Russia highlights its civilian nuclear power leadership, citing decades of experience with fast neutron reactors and progress toward a closed fuel cycle. It points to the BN-800’s shift to mixed uranium-plutonium fuel as advancing a two-component nuclear power industry.
entries.quote: Russia is the only country in the world that has accumulated significant experience in the commercial operation of fast neutron reactors with a focus on the development of closed fuel cycle technology. The first reactor became operative in the Soviet Union (Kazakh SSR) in 1973, the second in 1980, and the third in the Russian Federation in 2015. In September 2022, the BN-800 reactor at Beloyarsk NPP Unit 4 switched completely to mixed uranium-plutonium fuel. This is an important step in building a two-component nuclear power industry with a closed nuclear fuel cycle.

entries.position_summary: The country highlights large-scale training of international students in nuclear-related fields with ROSATOM’s assistance and expresses full support for the IAEA’s efforts to expand the peaceful use of nuclear energy for global well-being. This underscores a commitment to capacity-building and international cooperation in peaceful nuclear applications.
entries.quote: In the academic year 2021–2023 more than 2,000 foreign students in nuclear and related fields of study, recruited with the assistance of ROSATOM from 65 countries of the world, are enrolled in Russian universities We fully support IAEA efforts to promote the widespread use of nuclear energy for peace, health and well-being throughout the world.

entries.position_summary: Russia emphasizes cooperation with the IAEA on multilateral fuel-cycle approaches and assured supply mechanisms (e.g., INPRO and the International Uranium Enrichment Center with Kazakhstan) to expand access to peaceful nuclear energy while strengthening the NPT’s non-proliferation regime.
entries.quote: member states of the technical innovations and institutional features that ensure the sustainability of nuclear energy systems. 43 countries and the European Commission are involved in the INPRO project. Russia remains one of the leaders in joint work with the IAEA to develop the Concept of assured supplies and multilateral approaches to nuclear fuel cycle services. The concept contributes to the goals of developing and expanding the geography of peaceful uses of nuclear energy. At the same time, it strengthens the nuclear non-proliferation regime, the objective of the NPT. More specifically, the International Uranium Enrichment Center, established jointly with Kazakhstan, continues to operate in Russia.

entries.position_summary: Russia is promoting peaceful nuclear applications by signing an agreement to use a low-power nuclear power plant to supply energy for resource development in Yakutia. This reflects support for civilian nuclear energy to drive regional economic projects.
entries.quote: An agreement was signed for the supply and consumption of energy from a low-power nuclear power plant (SPPP) for the development of the Kyuchus deposit in the Ust-Yansky and Verkhoyansky districts of the Republic of Sakha (Yakutia).

entries.position_summary: Russia highlights its support for peaceful uses of nuclear technology by hosting and funding IAEA training events and making a voluntary contribution to the IAEA’s Rays of Hope cancer initiative.
entries.quote: Over six years, 24 training events organized by the Programme have been held in Russia, funded from Russian contributions and attended by more than 620 experts from 19 States members of the Agency. As part of the IAEA's Rays of Hope cancer- fighting initiative, in 2022, the Russian State Atomic Energy Corporation ROSATOM made a voluntary contribution of 100,000 Euros to support the 8

entries.position_summary: Russia emphasizes strong cooperation with the IAEA and states it provides political, financial, and expert support to the Agency, indicating support for IAEA safeguards and its broader activities, including peaceful uses. It positions itself as a contributor to the Agency’s work.
entries.quote: Russia attaches special importance to cooperation with the IAEA. We provide continuous political, financial and expert support to the Agency's activities.

entries.position_summary: The country highlights ongoing development of a lead-cooled fast reactor and on-site closed fuel cycle infrastructure to manage spent fuel and radioactive waste, aiming to improve nuclear power plant efficiency. This underscores its focus on advancing peaceful nuclear energy and enhancing safety/waste management capabilities.
entries.quote: We are continuing the construction of a pilot energy project with BREST- OD-300 lead-cooled reactor and the infrastructure necessary to close the nuclear fuel cycle within one site. This will enable us to fine-tune technologies addressing the issue of the spent nuclear fuel and radioactive waste and improve the efficiency of the nuclear power plants.
